MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT GLADYS DEWITT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant moves to strike allegations asserted in the Second, Third and Fourth causes of action (paragraphs 16, 19/50/65, and 21/51/53) forming the claims for wrongful eviction and tenant harassment. While paragraphs 16, 19 and 21 are incorporated into all causes of action, and allegations identical to paragraph 19 and 21 are separately made only within the second and third causes of action, Defendant moves to strike claims in the Fourth, but not in the First cause of action. As to the allegations in paragraphs 16, Defendant failed to carry its burden and show that any claims within the second, third and fourth causes of action (expressly subject to this motion) arise out of Defendant's activity described in this paragraph. Defendant further failed to carry its burden and show that any claims within the fourth cause of action arise out of any activities described in the paragraphs subject to this motion. Motion is granted as to paragraphs 19/50/65 and 21/51/53, except for the following language: "The police report was incident #200481579. The police refused to provide this report to Plaintiff because Plaintiff was not mentioned in incident report #200481579." Moving party carried its burden to show that claims within the second and the third causes of action arise out of protected activity. Opposing party failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits as litigation privilege applies to the activities as described in the Complaint and as supported by Plaintiff's evidence describing the activities in further detail and Plaintiff's description of the claims in its motion for leave to file supplemental complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
